input_text:
Project: QTWEBSITE
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Open
Summary: Qt documentation sometimes doesn't show up in searches
Description: Continuing on from QTWEBSITE-721, the search "rotationanimation qml" does not give me relevant results when searching online using startpage.com (which uses Google). There is only one doc.qt.io result, but it's for archived Qt 4.8 documentation. It should show the Qt 5 (or Qt 6; ideally, both) link: https://doc.qt.io/qt-6/qml-qtquick-rotationanimation.html
Comments:
1. Interestingly, both https://doc.qt.io/qt-6/qml-qtquick-rotationanimation.html and https://doc.qt.io/qt-5/qml-qtquick-rotationanimation.html are indexed by google, but don't show up when searching for the both terms that create the title. Maybe access to the Google site tools would tell us why this particular page got penaltized?
2. [i get https://doc.qt.io/qt-5/qml-qtquick-rotationanimator.html|https://doc.qt.io/qt-5/qml-qtquick-rotationanimator.html] for that search term on google as the second hit if I add qt 6 i get [https://doc.qt.io/qt-6/qml-qtquick-rotationanimation.html] as the first hit so it seems to be improving. is this sufficient?
3. I wouldn't call it sufficient as a user of Qt, personally. RotationAnimator is the wrong type.

expected.summary: This bug report says searches for "rotationanimation qml" do not surface the relevant Qt 5 or Qt 6 documentation page and instead return an archived Qt 4.8 result or the wrong type. The discussion notes that the correct pages are indexed, but the current search behavior is still not considered sufficient.
action_items: Investigate why the correct RotationAnimation documentation pages are not ranking properly in search results
Review the indexing and search visibility of the Qt 5 and Qt 6 RotationAnimation pages
risks: Users may be directed to archived or incorrect documentation pages instead of the relevant current documentation
open_questions: Would access to Google site tools explain why this particular page was penalized?
Is the recent improvement in search results sufficient?

expected.summary: Speaker D discusses debugging and stabilizing the summarization system, including how missing or non-word dialogue acts should be handled, how summaries and displays are behaving, and how the interface should be prepared for presentation. The conversation also covers evaluation plans, user documentation, sound-card issues on DICE machines, score scaling, agreement and disagreement indicators, and some interface behavior for search and display.
action_items: Check how the system handles dialogue acts with no words
Omit non-word items if they do not make sense for TF-IDF
Create a load box for selecting a file
Write a script that sets the class path so the system can be run easily
Add sanity checking to the code
Write user documentation or help files
Put the corrected code back into the group directory when it is ready
Implement the summarizer from the referenced paper
Handle sound-card issues so the system works on different machines
Add logic to detect whether the clock has loaded
Read agreement and disagreement scores into the system
Set a display threshold for agreement and disagreement indicators
Get contact details for the other group and discuss a joint evaluation
risks: Missing or non-word dialogue acts may distort TF-IDF or the summary
The current summary may be selecting the first items it sees rather than the best ones
Most DICE machines do not have working sound cards
The system may fail to load the clock on some machines
If skipping is not more accurate than the time steps, words could be cut off

Project: WEB
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Resolved
Summary: Purchasing Realm
Description: I haven been trying for the past week to buy a realm, i have tried different browsers, 4 different card and checking out with paypal, and i always get the same problem, i also had a friend try and buy it and same problem. Any help or ideas would be awesome.
Comments:
1. *Thank you for your report!* However, this issue is {color:#FF5722}*Invalid*{color} for the bug tracker. This is an account or billing issue. We do not have the tools to help you with this issue on this bug tracker. Please contact *[Mojang Support|https://help.minecraft.net/hc/en-us/requests/new]*. *Quick Links*: 📓 [Bug Tracker Guidelines|https://aka.ms/MCBugTrackerHelp] -- 💬 [Community Support|ht

expected.summary: This resolved bug report says the reporter was unable to purchase a Realm after trying multiple browsers, cards, PayPal, and even having a friend try. A comment explains that this is an account or billing issue and directs the user to Mojang Support rather than the bug tracker.
action_items: Contact Mojang Support for help with the Realm purchase issue
risks: The user cannot purchase a Realm through the normal checkout methods

expected.summary: The council approved an amendment to Council Bill 868 so the bill could be republished and reconsidered the following week rather than voted on that night. The discussion focused on clarifying through an added covenant or deed restriction that limits on above-ground construction over the parking lot property would carry forward with the transaction.
action_items: Amend Council Bill 868
Republish the bill before final adoption
Prepare the additional covenant or deed restriction clarifying the construction limitations on the parking lot property
Bring the bill back for final consideration the following week
risks: Without added clarification, the limitations on future construction over the parking lot property may remain unclear

The meeting then moved through the remaining consent and final consideration items, which were approved by block vote.

expected.summary: The council considered Council Bill 776, a right-of-way vacation request adjacent to 2999 Chestnut Place, and debated whether to deny it immediately or allow more time for the applicant and neighborhood to negotiate. After discussion about neighborhood concerns, state law, ongoing talks, and the possibility of a good neighbor agreement, the bill was ordered published and a public hearing was requested for the next stage.
action_items: Order Council Bill 776 published
Continue discussions between the applicant and the neighborhood
Present the proposed good neighbor agreement at the Wednesday meeting
Hold a public hearing when the bill returns
risks: The vacation would give up about 6000 square feet of right-of-way
There are concerns that the area is currently used for transportation purposes
Good neighbor agreements may not be enforceable by the city

input_text:
Project: ZOOKEEPER
Issue Type: Task
Status: Resolved
Summary: Fix Docker Hub Zookeeper-Versions to CVE-2021-44228
Description: As we are faced with critical [CVE-2021-44228|https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jfh8-c2jp-5v3q] (log4shell) these days, we still await security patches to fix log4j vulnerabilities published on December 12th, 2021. In our case we're running Apache Zookeeper via Docker, where unpatched versions still are available via the official Docker Image Repository. These images are shipped with jog4j and seem to not have recieved the critical security patches yet. e.g. v3.4.13: [https://hub.docker.com/layers/zookeeper/library/zookeeper/3.4.13/images/sha256-4ebfb9474e726f6b43674d8c3772bcda07a810d1c420196c69de3bc173c69e48?context=explore] When will these versions be updated in the Docker Repository to prevent users from being vulnerable with specific Zookeeper installations running?
Comments:
1. The linked docker image is not maintained by the Apache community - see https://hub.docker.com/_/zookeeper?tab=description You'll need to communicate with (see the link above) to get that resolved, ie: Maintained by: the Docker Community Where to get help: the Docker Community Forums, the Docker Community Slack, or Stack Overflow

expected.summary: This resolved task raises concern that official ZooKeeper Docker images still contained unpatched log4j vulnerabilities related to CVE-2021-44228. A comment clarifies that the linked Docker image is maintained by the Docker community, not the Apache community, so the issue must be addressed through Docker community support channels.
action_items: Contact the Docker community about the unpatched ZooKeeper Docker images
Use the Docker Community Forums, Docker Community Slack, or Stack Overflow to pursue a resolution
risks: Unpatched Docker images may leave ZooKeeper installations vulnerable to CVE-2021-44228

The ordinance included an exemption for applications that had an approved application on file by the close of business on September 8 and was also approved on a separate urgency vote so it could take effect immediately.

Conclude the hearing
Adopt the ordinance as read
Exempt applications with an approved application on file by the close of business on September 8
Approve the separate urgency vote so the ordinance takes effect immediately

Without the urgency vote, the ordinance would not take effect immediately

expected.summary: Speaker E argues that for the prototype the team should focus first on getting the interface working by plugging in ready-made or pre-annotated components and simple baselines where needed, then replace them later with better components. The discussion also covers using the NITE XML framework for synchronization and display, keeping most processing offline, deciding how to represent meetings and meeting series, and thinking about summarization and display levels.
action_items: Focus first on building a working prototype interface with pre-annotated data and simple baselines
Review the NITE XML framework in more detail to understand how its representation works
Decide what data representation the system should load and how everyone will access it
Keep calculations offline where possible and use the browser mainly as a viewer
Discuss whether meeting series should be represented as one entity with multiple levels of zoom
Check how the player works and whether utterance-level handling is preferable
risks: A complex multi-level representation could increase memory and implementation complexity
Using a custom framework instead of NITE would lose useful synchronization support
If representation choices are not settled early, interface integration may stall
open_questions: How should meeting series and individual meetings be represented in the interface?
Should summaries and information density be handled at word level or utterance level?
Can the full data be loaded at once without causing memory problems?

input_text:
Subject: Investment Structure

---------------------- Forwarded by Phillip K Allen/HOU/ECT on 09/26/138****0000:28 PM ---------------------------

"George Richards" <xxx@example.com> on 09/26/2000 01:18:45 PM
Please respond to <xxx@example.com>
To: "Phillip Allen" <xxx@example.com>
cc: "Larry Lewter" <xxx@example.com>, "Claudia L. Crocker" 
<xxx@example.com> 
Subject: Investment Structure

STRUCTURE:
Typically the structure is a limited partnership with a corporate (or LLC)
general partner.  The General Partner owns 1% of the project and carries the
liability of construction.

LAND OWNERSHIP & LOANS
The property would be purchased in the name of the limited partnership and
any land loans, land improvements loans and construction loans would be in
the name of the limited partnership.  Each of the individual investors and
all of the principals in Creekside would also personally guarantee the
loans.  If the investor(s) do not sign on the loans, this generally means
that a larger amount of cash is required and the investor's share of profits
is reduced.

All loans for residential construction, that are intended for re-sale, are
full recourse loans.  If we are pursuing multifamily rental developments,
the construction loans are still full recourse but the mortgage can often be
non-recourse.

USE OF INITIAL INVESTMENT
The initial investment is used for land deposit, engineering &
architectural design, soils tests, surveys, filing fees, legal fees for
organization and condominium association formation,  and appraisals.  Unlike
many real estate investment programs, none of the funds are used for fees to
Creekside Builders, LLC.  These professional expenses will be incurred over
the estimated 6 month design and approval period.

EARLY LAND COSTS
The $4,000 per month costs listed in the cash flow as part of land cost
represent the extension fees due to the seller for up to 4 months of
extensions on closing.  As an alternative, we can close into a land loan at
probably 70% of appraised value.  With a land value equal to the purchase
price of $680,000 this would mean a land loan of $476,000 with estimated
monthly interest payments of $3,966, given a 10% annual interest rate, plus
approximately 1.25% of the loan amount for closing costs and loan fees.

EQUITY AT IMPROVEMENT LOAN
Once the site plan is approved by the City of Austin, the City will require
the development entity to post funds for fiscal improvements, referred to as
the "fiscals".  This cost represents a bond for the completion of
improvements that COA considers vital and these funds are released once the
improvements have been completed and accepted by COA.  This release will be
for 90% of the cost with the remaining 10% released one year after
completion.  Releases can be granted once every 90 days and you should
expect that the release would occur 6 months after the start of lot
improvement construction.  These fiscals are usually posted in cash or an
irrevocable letter of credit.  As such, they have to be counted as a
development cost, even though they are not spent.  Because they are not
spent no interest is charged on these funds.

The lot improvement loan is typically 75% of the appraised value of a
finished lot, which I suspect will be at least $20,000 and potentially as
high as $25,000.  This would produce a loan amount of  $15,000 on $20,000
per lot.  With estimated per lot improvement costs of $9,000, 'fiscals' at
$2,000 and the land cost at $8,000 , total improved lot cost is $19,000
which means $0 to $4,000 per lot in total equity.  The investment prior to
obtaining the improvement loan would count towards any equity requirement
provided it was for direct costs.  Thus, the additional equity for the
improvement loan would be $0-$184,000.   Even if the maximum loan would
cover all costs, it is unlikely the bank would allow reimbursement of funds
spent. The higher estimates of equity investments are shown in the
preliminary proforma to be on the safe side.  The engineer is preparing a
tentative site layout with an initial evaluation of the phasing, which can
significantly reduce the cash equity requirement.

Phasing works as follows.  If the first phase was say 40 units, the total
lot improvement cost might average $31,000 per lot.   Of this, probably
$13,000 would be for improvements and $19,000 for the land cost.  The
improvements are higher to cover large one time up front costs for design
costs, the entry road, water treatment costs, perimeter fencing and
landscaping, and so on, as well as for 100% of the land.  The land loan for
undeveloped lots would be 70% of the appraised raw lot value, which I would
estimate as $10,000 per lot for a loan value of $7,000 per lot.  Then the
loan value for each improved lot would be $15,000 per lot.  This would give
you a total loan of $992,000, total cost of $1,232,645 for equity required
of $241,000.  This was not presented in the initial analysis as the phasing
is depended on a more careful assessment by the Civil Engineer as the
separate phases must each be able to stand on its own from a utility
standpoint.

CONSTRUCTION LOANS
There are three types of  construction loans.  First, is a speculative
(spec) loan that is taken out prior to any pre-sales activity.  Second,  is
a construction loan for a pre-sold unit, but the loan remains in the
builder/developers name.  Third, is a pre-sold unit with the construction
loan in the name of the buyer.  We expect to have up to 8 spec loans to
start the project and expect all other loans to be pre-sold units with loans
in the name of the builder/developer.  We do not expect to have any
construction loans in the name of the buyers, as such loans are too
difficult to manage and please new buyers unfamiliar with the process.

Spec loans will be for 70% to 75% of value and construction loans for
pre-sold units, if the construction loan is from the mortgage lender,  will
be from 80% to 95% of value.

DISBURSEMENTS
Disbursements will be handled by the General Partner to cover current and
near term third party costs, then to necessary reserves, then to priority
payments and then to the partners per the agreement.  The General Partner
will contract with Creekside Builders, LLC to construct the units and the
fee to CB will include a construction management and overhead fee equal to
15% of the direct hard cost excluding land, financing and sales costs.
These fees are the only monies to Creekside, Larry Lewter or myself prior to
calculation of profit, except for a) direct reimbursement for partnership
expenses and b) direct payment to CB for any subcontractor costs that it has
to perform.  For example, if CB cannot find a good trim carpenter sub, or
cannot find enough trim carpenters, etc., and it decides to undertake this
function, it will charge the partnership the same fee it was able to obtain
from third parties and will disclose those cases to the partnership.
Finally, CB will receive a fee for the use of any of its equipment if it is
used in lieu of leasing equipment from others.  At present CB does not own
any significant equipment, but it is considering the purchase of a sky track
to facilitate and speed up framing, cornice, roofing and drywall spreading.

REPORTING
We are more than willing to provide reports to track expenses vs. plan.
What did you have in mind?  I would like to use some form of internet based
reporting.

BOOKKEEPING
I am not sure what you are referring to by the question, "Bookkeeping
procedures to record actual expenses?"  Please expand.

INVESTOR INPUT
We are glad to have the investor's input on design and materials.  As always
the question will be who has final say if there is disagreement, but in my
experience I have always been able to reach consensus. As you, and I presume
Keith, want to be involved to learn as much as possible we would make every
effort to be accommodating.

CREEKSIDE PROCEEDURES
CB procedures for dealing with subs, vendors and professionals is not as
formal as your question indicates.  In the EXTREMELY tight labor market
obtaining 3 bids for each labor trade is not feasible.  For the professional
subs we use those with whom we have developed a previous rapport.  Finally,
for vendors they are constantly shopped.

PRE-SELECTED PROFESSIONALS, SUBS AND VENDORS
Yes there are many different subs that have been identified and I can
provide these if you are interested.

I know I have not answered everything, but this is a starting point.  Call
when you have reviewed and we can discuss further.

Sincerely,

George Richards
President, Creekside Builders, LLC

 - winmail.dat

expected.summary: This forwarded email outlines a proposed investment structure for a real estate project, including partnership structure, loan obligations, use of initial investment, equity requirements, construction loan types, disbursement rules, reporting, bookkeeping, investor input, and Creekside procedures. It explains that this is a starting point and invites further discussion after review.
action_items: Review the proposed investment structure and cash flow details
Discuss further questions after reviewing the information
Clarify what kind of internet-based reporting is desired
Expand on the bookkeeping procedures being requested
Request the list of pre-selected professionals, subcontractors, and vendors if interested
risks: Individual investors and Creekside principals would personally guarantee the loans
Residential construction loans intended for resale are full recourse loans
Additional equity may be required before the improvement loan is obtained
Phasing depends on a more careful civil engineering assessment
Obtaining three bids for each labor trade is not feasible in the tight labor market
open_questions: What kind of reporting is desired?
What is meant by "Bookkeeping procedures to record actual expenses?"
Are the investors interested in receiving the list of identified subs and vendors?

What is causing the large variation in average F0 values across meetings?

input_text:
Subject: San Juan Index

Liane,

 As we discussed yesterday, I am concerned there has been an attempt to 
manipulate the  El Paso San Juan monthly index.  A single buyer entered the 
marketplace on both September 26 and 27 and paid above market prices 
($4.70-$4.80) for San Juan gas with the intent to distort the index.  At the 
time of these trades, offers for physical gas at significantly (10 to 15 
cents) lower prices were bypassed in order to establish higher trades to 
report into the index calculation.  Additionally, these trades are out of 
line with the associated financial swaps for San Juan.

 We have compiled a list of financial and physical trades executed from 
September 25 to September 27.  These are the complete list of trades from 
Enron Online (EOL), Enron's direct phone conversations, and three brokerage 
firms (Amerex, APB, and Prebon).  Please see the attached spreadsheet for a 
trade by trade list and a summary.  We have also included a summary of gas 
daily prices to illustrate the value of San Juan based on several spread 
relationships.  The two key points from this data are as follows:

 1.  The high physical prices on the 26th & 27th (4.75,4,80) are much greater 
than the high financial trades (4.6375,4.665) on those days. 

 2.  The spread relationship between San Juan and other points (Socal & 
Northwest)  is  consistent between the end of September and 
       October gas daily.  It doesn't make sense to have monthly indeces that 
are dramatically different.

 I understand you review the trades submitted for outliers.  Hopefully, the 
trades submitted will reveal counterparty names and you will be able to 
determine that there was only one buyer in the 4.70's and these trades are 
outliers.  I wanted to give you some additional points of reference to aid in 
establishing a reasonable index.  It is Enron's belief that the trades at 
$4.70 and higher  were above market trades that should be excluded from the 
calculation of index.

 It is our desire to have reliable and accurate indeces against which to 
conduct our physical and financial business.  Please contact me 
anytime I can assist you towards this goal.

Sincerely,

Phillip Allen

expected.summary: Phillip tells Liane that he believes there was an attempt to manipulate the El Paso San Juan monthly index through above-market trades on September 26 and 27. He provides supporting trade and spread information and argues that trades at $4.70 and higher should be treated as outliers and excluded from the index calculation.
action_items: Review the attached spreadsheet of physical and financial trades
Use the submitted trade data to check for outliers and counterparties
Exclude trades at $4.70 and higher from the index calculation if they are confirmed to be above-market outliers
risks: The monthly index may have been distorted by above-market trades
Reliable and accurate indices could be undermined by including outlier trades

input_text:
Project: CONJ
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Closed
Summary: Saving values using Java 8 LocalTime does not store fractional parts of seconds
Description: When using {{java.time.LocalTime}} to store values in DB, {{org.mariadb.jdbc.BasePrepareStatement}} loses all the fractional parts of the seconds during the conversion to {{java.sql.Time}} and then uses that to store the value. Even if the conversion was done correctly, {{java.sql.Time}} only allows storing at millisecond precision, so some data would be lost in any case. There are proper implementations with correct fractional second support for OffsetDateTime, OffsetTime and ZonedDateTime so I assume this is just an oversight that LocalTime was not supported with fractions support? If it was intentional, then it would still make sense to keep at least millisecond precision which is also supported using the legacy java.sql.Time datatype. It is possible to work around the issue atm by implementing a custom {{org.mariadb.jdbc.internal.com.send.parameters.ParameterHolder}} and using that to set the value using {{org.mariadb.jdbc.BasePrepareStatement.setParameter()}}

expected.summary: This closed bug report says that saving values with Java 8 LocalTime loses the fractional parts of seconds when converting through java.sql.Time. The report notes that LocalTime appears to lack proper fractional second support, unlike other time-related types, and suggests a custom ParameterHolder as a workaround.
action_items: Investigate the loss of fractional seconds when storing java.time.LocalTime values
Add proper fractional second support for LocalTime
Preserve at least millisecond precision if full fractional support is not intended
risks: Fractional parts of seconds are lost when LocalTime values are stored
java.sql.Time only supports millisecond precision, so some data loss may still occur
open_questions: Was the lack of fractional second support for LocalTime intentional?

expected.summary: The council considered a further amendment to Council Bill 161 to update zoning code references, set an effective date, and clarify that certain site development applications already in the pipeline could continue under the prior small lot parking exemption. The amendment passed unanimously, after which the public hearing on the amended bill was opened and staff presented reasons for recommending denial of the broader text amendment while still emphasizing the need to address citywide parking and transportation issues.
action_items: Further amend Council Bill 161 with the corrected zoning code references and effective date
Allow eligible pending site development plan applications to continue using the prior small lot parking exemption
Open the public hearing on Council Bill 161 as amended
risks: The broader text amendment may create redevelopment and parking management challenges
There are concerns about balancing neighborhood parking impacts, affordable housing, and redevelopment on small lots

input_text: Agenda item two. Clip 4314495. Report of the City Clerk on the Certificate of Sufficiency for initiative number 134 concerning approving approval. Voting for mayor, city attorney and City Councilmember. Primary elections. Thank you. Clerk file 314495 is notice that initiative 134 has sufficient signatures to go to the ballot. According to the city charter, the city clerk has 20 days for receipt of notice from King County elections to file the notice with the city council. The city clerk filed her report and the certificate of sufficiency with the council before this clerk file on June 28th. This action started the 45 day clock for council action on the initiatives. In order to provide my colleagues with additional time to consider this initiative. I'm recommending holding this clerk file for another week. This clerk file will appear on every agenda until the City Council determines what action will be taken in response to initiative number 134 134. As a reminder, the city's election code C-11 is for code 2.04300. Prohibits elected officials and city employees from using their office for the promotion or opposition of any ballot measure. According to the code, we should refrain from discussing the merits of the initiative until we are actually voting on legislation to support or oppose the ballot proposition. That will occur at a future meeting and within 45 days of the clerk file of this clerk file being filed with the council. I recommend that Council members refrain from discussing this initiative today and until the Council considers legislation supported or opposing this ballot proposition. I move to postpone clerk file 314495 to July 12. Is there a second? Second. Thank you. It's been moved and seconded to postpone the Clarke file. Will the Clarke please call the roll on postponing of Clarke file 314495 until July 12th. Council member silent. Yes. Councilmember Strauss. Yes. Councilmember Herbold? Yes. Councilmember Lewis? Yes. Councilmember Morales. Yes. Councilmember Nelson. Yes. Councilmember Peterson. S Council president worries high it in favor and unopposed. Thank you. The motion carries the clerk file is postponed until July 12. This item will appear on every agenda until the City Council determines what action will be taken in response to Initiative 134. Madam Clerk, we go on to item number three.

expected.summary: The council considered clerk file 314495 regarding the certificate of sufficiency for Initiative 134 and noted that the filing started the 45-day period for council action. To allow more time for consideration and to comply with election code limits on discussing ballot measures, the council voted unanimously to postpone the clerk file until July 12 and keep it on each agenda until action is taken.
action_items: Postpone clerk file 314495 until July 12
Keep clerk file 314495 on every agenda until the council determines what action to take
Refrain from discussing the merits of Initiative 134 until the council considers legislation supporting or opposing the ballot proposition
risks: Council action on the initiative must occur within 45 days of the clerk file being filed
Elected officials and city employees are prohibited from using their office for the promotion or opposition of a ballot measure

Expected Behaviour * Bug does not reproduce on default release, Second Life 4.0.4 (314579) Apr 26 2016 17:54:49 (Second Life Release) * On default release the built in snapshot preview shows the correct image when saving at sizes other then current window. * See *Fig 3* attached showing default release snapshot preview set to "Large"
Comments:
1. Should be fixed with: https://bitbucket.org/NiranV/viewer-release-storm2118/commits/09d88674ef0cd190b4d2628694c92d8ed720ac0d
2. Testing on http://automated-builds-secondlife-com.s3.amazonaws.com/hg/repo/viewer-release-storm2118/rev/315848/index.html As far as I can tell from the shell trace, this is built at rev 4b90c85c222c, so includes this fix. 3. This change is indeed not intended behavior since it was meant to crop all pictures but this is by no means a negative effect, it allows saving a full screen shot to inventory whereas the cropping will always cut out the left/right of the interface for example. Which makes me think of adding an option to disallow cropping, which would allow having your entire UI on a smaller resolution picture, al

expected.summary: This story defect says the built-in snapshot preview is stretched when saving to inventory if the snapshot size is not set to the current window size, even though the popout preview and saved image are correct. Comments indicate a fix was expected in a specific commit, later testing suggested the main bug was fixed, and an additional behavior change may support saving uncropped full-screen images.
action_items: Fix the built-in snapshot preview so it displays correctly when the snapshot size is not set to the current window
Review the behavior change introduced by the fix when the snapshot size is set to current window
Consider adding an option to disallow cropping
risks: The built-in preview can show an incorrect stretched image
The fix introduced an unintended behavior change in another case
open_questions: Should there be an option to disallow cropping?

input_text:
Project: XD
Issue Type: Improvement
Status: To Do
Summary: SpringXD authenication and authorization using LDAP
Description: Hi, I'm using LDAP to a Windows Active Directory as the authentication method for SpringXD. But after the user is authenticated in the SpringXD, the user is not authorized to do anythin. Based on the guides and the configuration files, it is unclear where the authorization rules for each user should be put, ROLE_VIEW, ROLE_CREATE, ROLE_ADMIN. Can the SpringXD team guide me to where will the authorization rules be created? In SpringXD servers.yml file or in Active Directory? Regards, Yew Wee
Comments:
1. Please refer to the attached document. We will eventually get this added to the reference guide. Hope this helps.
2. Thanks Sabby. I tried structuring the AD to the document, with two OU of users and guides. But windows AD base users' DN on "CN=User1, OU=Users, OU=Company, DN=COMPANY, DC=SG" Currently it is unable to get the ROLE from the attribute CN in AD, because it is unable to search for the user in the group. My suspicion is because the SpringXD search based on "uid=User1,OU=Users,OU=Company, DN=COMPANY, D
3. Notice the following piece of code (https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-xd/blob/115487070e9196a4bb8bfd9b6fe8b85494ac6cca/spring-xd-dirt/src/main/java/org/springframework/xd/dirt/server/security/LdapAuthenticationConfiguration.java#L171) {code:java} ldapConfigurer.ldapAuthoritiesPopulator(new LdapAuthoritiesPopulator() { @Override public Collection getGrantedAuthorities(DirContextOperations u

expected.summary: This improvement request asks how SpringXD authorization should be configured when LDAP with Windows Active Directory is used for authentication. The reporter says authentication works, but authorization does not, and asks whether roles such as ROLE_VIEW, ROLE_CREATE, and ROLE_ADMIN should be defined in servers.yml or in Active Directory.
action_items: Clarify where authorization rules for LDAP users should be configured
Update the reference guide with the LDAP authorization guidance
Investigate why the Active Directory setup is not returning the expected roles
risks: Users may authenticate successfully but still be unable to do anything because authorization is not configured correctly
open_questions: Should the authorization rules be created in SpringXD servers.yml or in Active Directory?
Why is the current Active Directory setup unable to return the expected roles?

The debate focused on whether the land should remain public for possible transportation use or be vacated to support the proposed hotel project, with members weighing neighborhood opposition, the registered neighborhood organization's agreement, and the public value of the parcel.
action_items: Close the continued public hearing on Council Bill 776
Vote on whether to grant the requested right-of-way vacation
risks: Vacating the parcel could eliminate land that some members believe has current or future transportation value

input_text:
Subject: Investment Structure

---------------------- Forwarded by Phillip K Allen/HOU/ECT on 09/26/138****0000:26 PM ---------------------------

"George Richards" <xxx@example.com> on 09/26/2000 01:18:45 PM
Please respond to <xxx@example.com>
To: "Phillip Allen" <xxx@example.com>
cc: "Larry Lewter" <xxx@example.com>, "Claudia L. Crocker" 
<xxx@example.com> 
Subject: Investment Structure

STRUCTURE:
Typically the structure is a limited partnership with a corporate (or LLC)
general partner.  The General Partner owns 1% of the project and carries the
liability of construction.

LAND OWNERSHIP & LOANS
The property would be purchased in the name of the limited partnership and
any land loans, land improvements loans and construction loans would be in
the name of the limited partnership.  Each of the individual investors and
all of the principals in Creekside would also personally guarantee the
loans.  If the investor(s) do not sign on the loans, this generally means
that a larger amount of cash is required and the investor's share of profits
is reduced.

All loans for residential construction, that are intended for re-sale, are
full recourse loans.  If we are pursuing multifamily rental developments,
the construction loans are still full recourse but the mortgage can often be
non-recourse.

USE OF INITIAL INVESTMENT
The initial investment is used for land deposit, engineering &
architectural design, soils tests, surveys, filing fees, legal fees for
organization and condominium association formation,  and appraisals.  Unlike
many real estate investment programs, none of the funds are used for fees to
Creekside Builders, LLC.  These professional expenses will be incurred over
the estimated 6 month design and approval period.

EARLY LAND COSTS
The $4,000 per month costs listed in the cash flow as part of land cost
represent the extension fees due to the seller for up to 4 months of
extensions on closing.  As an alternative, we can close into a land loan at
probably 70% of appraised value.  With a land value equal to the purchase
price of $680,000 this would mean a land loan of $476,000 with estimated
monthly interest payments of $3,966, given a 10% annual interest rate, plus
approximately 1.25% of the loan amount for closing costs and loan fees.

EQUITY AT IMPROVEMENT LOAN
Once the site plan is approved by the City of Austin, the City will require
the development entity to post funds for fiscal improvements, referred to as
the "fiscals".  This cost represents a bond for the completion of
improvements that COA considers vital and these funds are released once the
improvements have been completed and accepted by COA.  This release will be
for 90% of the cost with the remaining 10% released one year after
completion.  Releases can be granted once every 90 days and you should
expect that the release would occur 6 months after the start of lot
improvement construction.  These fiscals are usually posted in cash or an
irrevocable letter of credit.  As such, they have to be counted as a
development cost, even though they are not spent.  Because they are not
spent no interest is charged on these funds.

The lot improvement loan is typically 75% of the appraised value of a
finished lot, which I suspect will be at least $20,000 and potentially as
high as $25,000.  This would produce a loan amount of  $15,000 on $20,000
per lot.  With estimated per lot improvement costs of $9,000, 'fiscals' at
$2,000 and the land cost at $8,000 , total improved lot cost is $19,000
which means $0 to $4,000 per lot in total equity.  The investment prior to
obtaining the improvement loan would count towards any equity requirement
provided it was for direct costs.  Thus, the additional equity for the
improvement loan would be $0-$184,000.   Even if the maximum loan would
cover all costs, it is unlikely the bank would allow reimbursement of funds
spent. The higher estimates of equity investments are shown in the
preliminary proforma to be on the safe side.  The engineer is preparing a
tentative site layout with an initial evaluation of the phasing, which can
significantly reduce the cash equity requirement.

Phasing works as follows.  If the first phase was say 40 units, the total
lot improvement cost might average $31,000 per lot.   Of this, probably
$13,000 would be for improvements and $19,000 for the land cost.  The
improvements are higher to cover large one time up front costs for design
costs, the entry road, water treatment costs, perimeter fencing and
landscaping, and so on, as well as for 100% of the land.  The land loan for
undeveloped lots would be 70% of the appraised raw lot value, which I would
estimate as $10,000 per lot for a loan value of $7,000 per lot.  Then the
loan value for each improved lot would be $15,000 per lot.  This would give
you a total loan of $992,000, total cost of $1,232,645 for equity required
of $241,000.  This was not presented in the initial analysis as the phasing
is depended on a more careful assessment by the Civil Engineer as the
separate phases must each be able to stand on its own from a utility
standpoint.

CONSTRUCTION LOANS
There are three types of  construction loans.  First, is a speculative
(spec) loan that is taken out prior to any pre-sales activity.  Second,  is
a construction loan for a pre-sold unit, but the loan remains in the
builder/developers name.  Third, is a pre-sold unit with the construction
loan in the name of the buyer.  We expect to have up to 8 spec loans to
start the project and expect all other loans to be pre-sold units with loans
in the name of the builder/developer.  We do not expect to have any
construction loans in the name of the buyers, as such loans are too
difficult to manage and please new buyers unfamiliar with the process.

Spec loans will be for 70% to 75% of value and construction loans for
pre-sold units, if the construction loan is from the mortgage lender,  will
be from 80% to 95% of value.

DISBURSEMENTS
Disbursements will be handled by the General Partner to cover current and
near term third party costs, then to necessary reserves, then to priority
payments and then to the partners per the agreement.  The General Partner
will contract with Creekside Builders, LLC to construct the units and the
fee to CB will include a construction management and overhead fee equal to
15% of the direct hard cost excluding land, financing and sales costs.
These fees are the only monies to Creekside, Larry Lewter or myself prior to
calculation of profit, except for a) direct reimbursement for partnership
expenses and b) direct payment to CB for any subcontractor costs that it has
to perform.  For example, if CB cannot find a good trim carpenter sub, or
cannot find enough trim carpenters, etc., and it decides to undertake this
function, it will charge the partnership the same fee it was able to obtain
from third parties and will disclose those cases to the partnership.
Finally, CB will receive a fee for the use of any of its equipment if it is
used in lieu of leasing equipment from others.  At present CB does not own
any significant equipment, but it is considering the purchase of a sky track
to facilitate and speed up framing, cornice, roofing and drywall spreading.

REPORTING
We are more than willing to provide reports to track expenses vs. plan.
What did you have in mind?  I would like to use some form of internet based
reporting.

BOOKKEEPING
I am not sure what you are referring to by the question, "Bookkeeping
procedures to record actual expenses?"  Please expand.

INVESTOR INPUT
We are glad to have the investor's input on design and materials.  As always
the question will be who has final say if there is disagreement, but in my
experience I have always been able to reach consensus. As you, and I presume
Keith, want to be involved to learn as much as possible we would make every
effort to be accommodating.

CREEKSIDE PROCEEDURES
CB procedures for dealing with subs, vendors and professionals is not as
formal as your question indicates.  In the EXTREMELY tight labor market
obtaining 3 bids for each labor trade is not feasible.  For the professional
subs we use those with whom we have developed a previous rapport.  Finally,
for vendors they are constantly shopped.

PRE-SELECTED PROFESSIONALS, SUBS AND VENDORS
Yes there are many different subs that have been identified and I can
provide these if you are interested.

I know I have not answered everything, but this is a starting point.  Call
when you have reviewed and we can discuss further.

Sincerely,

George Richards
President, Creekside Builders, LLC

 - winmail.dat

expected.summary: This forwarded email outlines a proposed investment structure for a real estate project, covering partnership structure, loans, equity requirements, construction financing, disbursement procedures, reporting, bookkeeping, investor input, and Creekside procedures. It presents the information as a starting point and invites further discussion after review.
action_items: Review the proposed investment structure and financing details
Discuss further questions after reviewing the information
Clarify what kind of internet-based reporting is desired
Expand on what is meant by bookkeeping procedures to record actual expenses
Request the list of identified professionals, subcontractors, and vendors if interested
risks: Individual investors and Creekside principals would personally guarantee the loans
Residential construction loans intended for resale are full recourse loans
Additional equity may be required before obtaining the improvement loan
Phasing depends on further assessment by the civil engineer
Obtaining three bids for each labor trade is not feasible in the current labor market
open_questions: What kind of reporting is desired?
What is meant by "Bookkeeping procedures to record actual expenses?"
Is there interest in receiving the list of identified subs and vendors?

input_text: The report of the City Council Agenda Item one Quick File 314495. Report of the City Clerk on the set of the on the Certificate of Sufficiency for initiative number 134. Thank you. CLERK 5314495. As notice of Initiative 134 is sufficient signatures to go on the ballot, according to the city charter. The city clerk has 20 days from receipt of notice from King County elections to file the notice with the city council. The clerk city clerk filed a report and certificate of sufficiency with the Council via this clerk file on June 28. This action started the 45 day clock for council action on the initiative in order to provide my colleagues with additional time to consider this initiative, recommending holding the clerk file for another week until Thursday, this court file will appear on every agenda until the City Council determines what action will be taken in response to initiative number 134. As a reminder, the City Council City's Election Code, SC 2.0 4.300 prohibits elected officials and city employees from using their office for the promotion or opposition of any ballot measure. According to the code. We should refrain from discussing the merits of the initiative until we are actually voting on the legislation to support or oppose the ballot proposition that will occur at a future meeting and within 45 days of this court file 314495 being filed with the Council, I recommend that councils refrain from discussing this initiative today . And until the Council considers legislation supporting or opposing this ballot measure ballot proposition, I move to postpone clerk file 3144952 July 14th. Is there a second second? It has been moved and seconded to postpone the clerk filed with the clerk. Please call the roll on postponement clerk file 314495 until July 14th. Councilmember Herbold Yes. Councilmember Lewis Yes. Councilmember Morales Yes. Councilmember Mosqueda i. E. Councilmember Nelson I. Councilmember Peterson Yes. Councilmember Sawant. Yes. Council President Pro Tem Strauss. Yes. Eight in favor and unopposed. Thank you. The motion carries a quick vote is postponed to July 14, 2022, and this item will appear on every agenda until the City Council determines what action will be taken in response to initiative ember 134 Item number two Will Clerk please read item two into the record. An ordinance relating to prosecuting violations of domestic violence and other protection orders to make the Seattle Municipal Code consistent with state law. Amending Section 12 .8.09.020 about Seattle Municipal Code.

expected.summary: The council considered clerk file 314495 regarding the certificate of sufficiency for Initiative 134 and noted that the filing triggered a 45-day clock for council action. To allow more time for consideration and to comply with election code restrictions on discussing ballot measures, the council voted unanimously to postpone the clerk file until July 14, 2022.
action_items: Postpone clerk file 314495 until July 14, 2022
Refrain from discussing the merits of Initiative 134 until the council considers legislation supporting or opposing the ballot measure
Keep clerk file 314495 on every agenda until the council determines what action to take
risks: Council action on the initiative must occur within 45 days of the clerk file being filed
Elected officials and city employees are prohibited from using their office for the promotion or opposition of any ballot measure

expected.summary: The council postponed consideration of Council Bill 285 for one week, then turned to Council Bill 161 and approved a technical amendment to close a zoning loophole related to gross floor area and dwelling units. The council then adopted additional amendments emphasizing transportation demand management and requiring informational notice for certain small lot parking exemptions before ordering Council Bill 161 published as amended.
action_items: Postpone Council Bill 285 for one week until Tuesday, March 28th
Amend Council Bill 161 to correct the technical language regarding gross floor area and dwelling units
Amend Council Bill 161 to include preamble language on transportation demand management
Amend Council Bill 161 to require a zoning permit with informational notice for certain new buildings on small zone lots
Order Council Bill 161 published as amended
risks: There was concern about a loophole in the ordinance text related to parking requirements
There were concerns about neighborhood impacts and the need for better transportation demand management
Administering future transportation demand management programs may require significant resources

Summary: Upgrade jboss-parent to 36

expected.summary: This open component upgrade request says jboss-parent 35 uses an unbounded JDK version range in its profiles, which prevents Ant from building XNIO. The report notes that the issue was fixed in jboss-parent 36 and requests upgrading to that version.
action_items: Upgrade jboss-parent to version 36
risks: The current jboss-parent 35 configuration prevents Ant from building because of an invalid unbounded JDK version range

B also offers to adapt existing code to generate per-meeting word files ordered by time and send a sample file for review.

Adapt the existing code to output sequences of words instead of durations

What exactly needs to be demonstrated?

expected.summary: This open bug report says the ZooKeeper server exits with a transaction ordering error where it is committing zxid 0x100000003 while the next pending transaction is 0x100000002. The report includes startup and server logs showing the failure context.
action_items: Investigate the transaction ordering mismatch causing the server to exit
Review the provided server logs to identify why the pending transaction zxid is behind the committing zxid
risks: The server exits because of a transaction ordering inconsistency

input_text:
Project: WLC
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Done
Summary: Many occurrences of "Cannot request user details from host without an authenticated user context" in log
Description: The log message "{{Cannot request user details from host without an authenticated user context}}" occurs when the display name cache of a user expires, and no OAuth-authenticated request prompting a cache refresh is received. Consequently, all polling requests get the cache miss and fail to issue the request. We can now fix this trivially with the addition of a [method to execute signed requests as a given user|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-connect-play-java/commits/a1ed46a4cf1d83c9e9a431c5d76b26e61eb3624a] to ac-play-java.
Comments:
1. Fixed in 1.6 (no need for descriptor update).

expected.summary: This completed bug report says the log message "Cannot request user details from host without an authenticated user context" occurred when a user's display name cache expired and polling requests could not refresh it. The issue was made easy to fix after ac-play-java added a method to execute signed requests as a given user.
action_items: Use the new ac-play-java method to execute signed requests as a given user
Fix the cache refresh behavior so polling requests can obtain user details when the display name cache expires
risks: Expired display name cache entries can cause repeated polling failures and log noise

input_text:
Project: TFSAP
Issue Type: New Feature
Status: Open
Summary: Add built-in task to get Build Info and/or properties from Artifact
Description: When promoting a docker image you need to specify the build info of the image you want to promote. There is currently no built-in way to get build info from an existing Artifact. The only way of getting build info from an existing Artifact in Artifactory is by using the REST API and getting the `properties` of an artifact. Even your youtube video shows the usage of cURL in the process of promoting a docker image: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3UaqJGXMp8w I would like some addition to `Artifactory.server` so you can call something like: {code:java} - task: ArtifactoryGetBuildInfo@1 displayName: 'Get Build Info From Artifactory' inputs: artifactoryService: 'Artifactory Widex' artifactName: 'someArtifact' repository: 'docker-snapshot-local/cool_Image:coolerTag' variableToSave: 'image_build_info'{code} where `variableToSave` is the name of the variable that will contain the build info. Maybe the same functionality for getting `properties` of an artifact.

expected.summary: This feature request asks for a built-in task to retrieve build info and possibly artifact properties directly from an existing artifact in Artifactory. The requester wants this added to Artifactory.server so promoting a Docker image does not require calling the REST API or using cURL.
action_items: Add a built-in task to get build info from an existing artifact
Add support for retrieving artifact properties from an existing artifact
Extend Artifactory.server with functionality similar to the proposed ArtifactoryGetBuildInfo task
risks: Users currently have to rely on the REST API or cURL to get build info from an existing artifact

risks: Users may be unable to access expected .sha256 files immediately after release due to synchronization lag

input_text:
Project: CONJ
Issue Type: Task
Status: Open
Summary: Connection Redirection Mechanism implementation
Description: _emphasized text_The goal is to implement proxy redirection protocol describe in MDEV-15935 : Connection process would ends with [OK_Packet |https://mariadb.com/kb/en/ok_packet/] that may indicate redirection mechanism with the format "Location: mysql:// : /key=value&key2=value2" in `info` field. Connector, if having an option that permits redirection, must then redirect to the indicated location and close initial socket. A successful redirection will be cached for some time (next 10 minutes). If some following connection establishment use a cache location and fail to connect, the connector will then try to reconnect to initial host silently.

expected.summary: This task is to implement the proxy redirection protocol described in MDEV-15935. The connector should redirect to the location provided in the OK_Packet info field, cache a successful redirection for 10 minutes, and silently reconnect to the initial host if a cached redirection fails.
action_items: Implement the proxy redirection protocol described in MDEV-15935
Redirect to the indicated location when the connector option permits redirection
Close the initial socket after redirection
Cache a successful redirection for 10 minutes
Reconnect to the initial host silently if a cached redirection fails
risks: A cached redirection may fail to connect, requiring fallback to the initial host

expected.summary: The council discussed whether Council Bill 522, concerning the Colorado Symphony's lease, should proceed or be sent back to committee. Members raised concerns about increasing the Symphony's rent, the financial impact on the organization, and the broader future of the building, and the motion to hold the bill in committee until August 24, 2015 passed.
action_items: Hold Council Bill 522 in committee until Monday, August 24th, 2015
Bring the Colorado Symphony representatives into committee for further discussion
Review the building's future use and planning
Review the financial impact of the lease on the Colorado Symphony
risks: The rent increase could impose financial strain on the Colorado Symphony
Further investment in the building could complicate future repurposing or redevelopment
Delays or changes could create timing issues before the current lease expires

input_text:
Subject: Re:

Traveling to have a business meeting takes the fun out of the trip.  Especially if you have to prepare a presentation.  I would suggest holding the business plan meetings here then take a trip without any formal business meetings.  I would even try and get some honest opinions on whether a trip is even desired or necessary.

As far as the business meetings, I think it would be more productive to try and stimulate discussions across the different groups about what is working and what is not.  Too often the presenter speaks and the others are quiet just waiting for their turn.   The meetings might be better if held in a round table discussion format.  

My suggestion for where to go is Austin.  Play golf and rent a ski boat and jet ski's.  Flying somewhere takes too much time.

expected.summary: The email suggests holding business plan meetings locally instead of combining them with travel and recommends getting honest input on whether a trip is necessary at all. It also suggests using a round table discussion format for business meetings and recommends Austin as a travel destination if a trip is still desired.
action_items: Hold the business plan meetings locally instead of during a trip
Get honest opinions on whether a trip is desired or necessary
Use a round table discussion format for the business meetings
Consider Austin as the destination if a trip is planned
risks: Traveling for a business meeting may reduce the value and enjoyment of the trip
Formal presentations may make the meetings less productive by limiting discussion
open_questions: Is a trip even desired or necessary?

expected.summary: Speaker C discusses how to pass topic-segment data into a word-importance process, how values might be attached back to utterances or XML attributes, and the limitations of using Rainbow for this task. The conversation explores splitting data by topic boundaries, matching words back into segments, comparing different measures, and using XML parsing and timestamps to reconnect calculated values to the original structure.
action_items: Decide how the data file should be structured for topic segments
Determine whether to attach new values as attributes in the existing XML structure
Investigate whether Rainbow can return values in a usable order
Consider writing a custom solution instead of relying on Rainbow
Use XML parsing and timestamps to map calculated values back to segments
risks: Rainbow may reorder words by information gain and make it hard to map values back to the original positions
Bag-of-words processing may lose the ordering needed to reconstruct segment-level values
open_questions: Should values be attached per word, per utterance, or per segment?
Can Rainbow provide the values in the right order for writing them back?
What is the right function for combining the different measures?
Is laughter annotated and can it be filtered out?

Mr. ENGLISH And further informed my office that he was actually selling the business and that it was currently in escrow. Councilman Orson Just 1/2. In talking to the city attorney, I know I called public comment and no one came forward, but just to make sure the applicant does not want to say anything, is that correct? Does the police department have any information as to whether there are any pending applications with ABC to change the ownership of the ABC license? No, not at this time. Okay. So is Mr. Patrick Collins still listed as the managing member of Shamrock Hospitality Group, the owner of Modo Saloon and Art? And are there any pending applications with ABC to change that designation? No. There are no attempts right now to change that. As far as any research that I was able to find in referencing the California Department of Alcoholic Beverage Control License CORI System as of February 18th, 2019, Mr. Patrick Conlin is listed as one of the owners managing member and there are two additional owners listed on the application with California Department of Alcoholic Beverage Control. And to your knowledge, has business licensing received any applications or inquest inquiries from a potential new owner about an entertainment permit for modern saloon? No, we have. My office has not heard anything from any new potential owner of Muldoon's. I appreciate the work that has been done to address the concerns raised by the community back in December and that he is now in the process of selling the business. However, considering that the entertainment permit is specific to the applicant and the new owner would have to apply for a new entertainment permit based on Mr. English's representations to my office about the status of the sale, and that the new ownership would take ownership as soon as this week requiring a new entertainment permit application, and considering that despite representations otherwise last September, there have been no indications of any official change of the current ownership structure in either the city's or state's records. To address the concerns that have been initially raised, I would have to move to support received the supporting documentation into the record, conclude the public hearing and deny the entertainment permit for Shamrock Hospitality Group doing business as Muldoon Saloon. expected.summary: The council held a hearing on the application for an Entertainment Without Dancing permit for Muldoon's Saloon and reviewed its history of continuances, neighborhood concerns, and questions about ownership changes. After hearing from staff and council, the council voted to receive the documentation, conclude the hearing, and deny the entertainment permit.
action_items: Receive the supporting documentation into the record
Conclude the public hearing
Deny the entertainment permit for Shamrock Hospitality Group doing business as Muldoon's Saloon
risks: There were prior concerns about unpermitted bands and neighborhood issues
The ownership status of the business remained unclear in city and state records
Any new owner would still need to apply for a new entertainment permit

input_text:
Subject: Consolidated positions: Issues & To Do list

---------------------- Forwarded by Phillip K Allen/HOU/ECT on 10/09/138****0000:00 PM ---------------------------

Richard Burchfield
10/06/2000 06:59 AM
To: Phillip K Allen/HOU/ECT@ECT
cc: Beth Perlman/HOU/ECT@ECT 
Subject: Consolidated positions: Issues & To Do list

Phillip,
 Below is the issues & to do list as we go forward with documenting the 
requirements for consolidated physical/financial positions and transport 
trade capture. What we need to focus on is the first bullet in Allan's list; 
the need for a single set of requirements. Although the meeting with Keith, 
on Wednesday,  was informative the solution of creating a infinitely dynamic 
consolidated position screen, will be extremely difficult and time 
consuming.  Throughout the meeting on Wednesday, Keith alluded to the 
inability to get consensus amongst the traders on the presentation of the 
consolidated position, so the solution was to make it so that a trader can 
arrange the position screen to their liking (much like Excel). What needs to 
happen on Monday from 3 - 5 is a effort to design a desired layout for the 
consolidated position screen, this is critical. This does not exclude 
building a capability to create a more flexible position presentation for the 
future, but in order to create a plan that can be measured we need firm 
requirements. Also, to reiterate that the goals of this project is a project 
plan on consolidate physical/financial positions and transport trade capture. 
The other issues that have been raised will be capture as projects on to 
themselves, and will need to be prioritised as efforts outside of this 
project.

I have been involved in most of the meetings and the discussions have been 
good. I believe there has been good communication between the teams, but now 
we need to have focus on the objectives we set out to solve.

Richard  
---------------------- Forwarded by Richard Burchfield/HOU/ECT on 10/06/138****0000:34 AM ---------------------------

Allan Severude
10/05/2000 06:03 PM
To: Richard Burchfield/HOU/ECT@ECT
cc: Peggy Alix/HOU/ECT@ECT, Russ Severson/HOU/ECT@ECT, Scott 
Mills/HOU/ECT@ECT, Kenny Ha/HOU/ECT@ECT 
Subject: Consolidated positions: Issues & To Do list

From our initial set of meetings with the traders regarding consolidated 
positions, I think we still have the following issues:
We don't have a single point of contact from the trading group.  We've had 
three meetings which brought out very different issues from different 
traders.  We really need a single point of contact to help drive the trader 
requirements and help come to a consensus regarding the requirements.
We're getting hit with a lot of different requests, many of which appear to 
be outside the scope of position consolidation.

Things left to do:
I think it may be useful to try to formulate a high level project goal to 
make it as clear as possible what we're trying to accomplish with this 
project.  It'll help determine which requests fall under the project scope.
Go through the list of requests to determine which are in scope for this 
project and which fall out of scope.
For those in scope, work to define relative importance (priority) of each and 
work with traders to define the exact requirements of each.
Define the desired lay out of the position manager screen: main view and all 
drill downs.
Use the above to formulate a project plan.

Things requested thus far (no particular order):
Inclusion of Sitara physical deals into the TDS position manager and deal 
ticker.
Customized rows and columns in the position manager (ad hoc rows/columns that 
add up existing position manager rows/columns).
New drill down in the position manager to break out positions by: physical, 
transport, swaps, options, ...
Addition of a curve tab to the position manager to show the real-time values 
of all curves on which the desk has a position.
Ability to split the current position grid to allow daily positions to be 
shown directly above monthly positions.  Each grouped column in the top grid 
would be tied to a grouped column in the bottom grid.
Ability to properly show curve shift for float-for-float deals; determine the 
appropriate positions to show for each:
Gas Daily for monthly index,
Physical gas for Nymex,
Physical gas for Inside Ferc,
Physical gas for Mid market.
Ability for TDS to pull valuation results based on a TDS flag instead of 
using official valuations.
Position and P&L aggregation across all gas desks.
Ability to include the Gas Price book into TDS:
Inclusion of spread options in our systems.  Ability to handle volatility 
skew and correlations.
Ability to revalue all options incrementally throughout the trading day.  
Approximate delta changes between valuations using instantaneous gamma or a 
gamma grid.
Valuation of Gas Daily options.
A new position screen for options (months x strike x delta).  TBD.
Inclusion of positions for exotic options currently managed in spreadsheets.
Ability to isolate the position change due to changed deals in the position 
manager.
Ability to view change deal P&L in the TDS deal ticker.  Show new deal terms, 
prior deal terms, and net P&L affect of the change.
Eliminate change deals with no economic impact from the TDS deal ticker.
Position drill down in the position manager to isolate the impact of 
individual deals on the position total in a grid cell.
Benchmark positions in TDS.
Deployment of TDS in Canada. Currency and volume uom conversions. Implicit 
and explicit position break out issues.

-- Allan.

PS: Colleen is setting up a meeting tomorrow to discuss the direction for 
transport.  Hopefully we'll know much better where that part stands at that 
point.

expected.summary: This forwarded email says the consolidated positions project needs a single set of requirements and a defined layout for the consolidated position screen before a measurable project plan can be created. It also lists open scope and requirements issues, along with many requested features that should be prioritized or treated as separate efforts if they fall outside the project scope.
action_items: Focus on defining a single set of requirements for consolidated positions
Design the desired layout for the consolidated position screen during the Monday meeting from 3 to 5
Formulate a high-level project goal
Review the request list to determine which items are in scope and which are out of scope
Define the priority and exact requirements of the in-scope items
Use the agreed requirements and layout to formulate a project plan
risks: There is no single point of contact from the trading group
Different traders have raised very different issues and there is no consensus on requirements
Many requests appear to be outside the scope of the project
An infinitely dynamic consolidated position screen would be extremely difficult and time consuming

expected.summary: Speaker B discusses how to structure the report, screenshots, future work, and deadlines for topic segmentation and speaker characterization. The conversation also covers search result windows, extracting timestamps and attributes from NITE data, how to handle disfluencies and XML output, and how to organize the written report sections.

Have Pernilla review or refine the relevant section
Add future work to the conclusion of the final report
Tune the topic segmentation parameters
Check how the underlying data for the inverted file is progressing
Extract the start and end time attributes from the NOM write elements
Decide how to handle disfluency removal and XML output
Use NITE tools rather than custom Java XML handling where possible
Organize the written report into coherent sections
risks: There may not be enough time to complete topic segmentation and speaker characterization fully
Writing processed results back into XML could be difficult
Using plain text instead of XML may limit what can be done with the data
open_questions: Should the report show screenshots individually or grouped together?
How should the different search results be shown in the interface?
How will processed disfluency data be written back into XML?

expected.summary: Speaker C discusses how different components of the system should connect, how search and meeting data should be loaded and presented, and whether architecture should remain modular. The discussion also covers loading corpora, caching and performance concerns, possible search workflows, ranking, frequencies, and ideas for startup and browse options.
action_items: Upload the updated version after changing the file loading action
Wire in the topics and summaries
Find out what object types are used for the other components
Add a check to force loading a meeting when needed
Discuss the search and ranking approach with Pernilla
Consider adding startup options for search and browse tasks
risks: Loading multiple corpora or NOM objects for search results could be time-consuming
The first load appears slow and repeated debugging may be cumbersome
The frequency-based approach previously tried was not working properly
open_questions: Is there a standard control for connecting topics and highlighted text?
Will search require loading a new corpus or NOM object for each returned meeting?
Do they actually need frequencies or TF-IDF for the planned functionality?
Should users be forced to load a meeting before searching?
Should results be ranked?
What should appear on the startup screen?

input_text:
Project: STORM
Issue Type: Defect
Status: Closed
Summary: Missing notification NoVoiceConnect-GIAB on QucikGraphics cohort
Description: Non system grid voice server has failure logic and noticification call in llvivoxvoice but no corresponding entry in notifications.xml. This change adds the notification for NoVoiceConnect-GIAB.
Comments:
1. If this has been tended to I suppose it can be closed.
2. Can you explain why the different notice was needed? As far as I can see it's very close to identitical to NoVoiceConnect
3. Ref: http://jira.phoenixviewer.com/browse/FIRE-13130 "Unknown Notification Message ,,, 'NoVoiceConnect-GIAB' was not found..." I think this missing notification message will only happen on OpenSim grids.

expected.summary: This closed defect adds a missing notification entry for NoVoiceConnect-GIAB because the voice server failure logic referenced it in code but there was no corresponding entry in notifications.xml. A comment suggests this missing notification would likely occur only on OpenSim grids.
action_items: Add the missing NoVoiceConnect-GIAB entry to notifications.xml
risks: The missing notification message may occur on OpenSim grids
The code referenced a notification that did not exist in notifications.xml
open_questions: Why was a different notice needed instead of using NoVoiceConnect?

input_text:
Project: STORM
Issue Type: Story
Status: Closed
Summary: Camera presets
Description: Camera presets will operate in a similar way to graphics presets. It will save and restore settings in the Camera Controls floater. Certainly: - Orbit angle (round control on Camera Controls) - Pan location (square control on Camera Controls) - Zoom - View angle Possible additional features: - Controls related to depth of field Preferences should also include (but not save as part of a camera preset): - Opacity control for the Camera Controls floater The existing Front/Side/Rear should be put into the default camera presets, and also a "Pennys Gamer Default" Also fixed in these changes: BUG-10398 BUG-17348 BUG-17349
Comments:
1. How about we also replace SL's old default camera? Sounds like with the improved interface we'll be able to keep it as an alternate setting, but those settings were created in the 90's by Lindens with no game design experience, and they've caused a lot of problems for environment design in SL by forcing creators to build every interior room as if it were a stadium. I've attached some alternate cam
2. We should be able to what Penny suggests
3. If you mean update the default, please do. Those of us making more RL scale content have to constantly educate customers about how not to have their camera flying up behind them like a kite, and getting stuck behind walls.

expected.summary: This closed story adds camera presets that work similarly to graphics presets by saving and restoring camera control settings. It also proposes default presets, possible depth-of-field support, and includes fixes for BUG-10398, BUG-17348, and BUG-17349.
action_items: Implement camera presets that save and restore camera control settings
Include orbit angle, pan location, zoom, and view angle in the presets
Consider adding controls related to depth of field
Include opacity control for the Camera Controls floater in preferences
Add the existing Front, Side, and Rear settings to the default camera presets
Add a "Pennys Gamer Default" preset
open_questions: Should the old default camera also be replaced or kept as an alternate setting?

Treat a series of meetings as one continuous display with hard boundaries between meetings
Implement a list-based similar-segment search result view
Come up with names or short labels for segments
Meet later to continue integrating the display and search behavior
risks: The display and audio may fall out of sync if external processing is slow
The current prototype still uses old data and made-up segments in some places
Adding full multi-meeting behavior may add complexity without much presentation benefit
open_questions: Should summarization always compress by a fixed factor or to a fixed duration?
Is it worth displaying whole meeting series as one continuous item?
How should similar-segment search results be named and shown to the user?

input_text:
Project: CONJ
Issue Type: Task
Status: Closed
Summary: Permit suppression of resultset metadata getTableName for oracle compatibility
Description: The oracle.jdbc.OracleResultSetMetaData interface does not implement the getSchemaName and getTableName methods because Oracle Database does not make this feasible anymore. For compatibility, with oracle result, a new option to suppress resultset metadata schema/table (and aliases) can be added.

expected.summary: This closed task proposes adding an option to suppress result set metadata schema and table names, including aliases, for Oracle compatibility. The reason given is that Oracle's OracleResultSetMetaData interface does not implement getSchemaName and getTableName.
action_items: Add an option to suppress result set metadata schema and table names, including aliases, for Oracle compatibility

expected.summary: Speaker B suggests that the group work together to understand the XML format so their annotations can be structured correctly and integrated consistently. The discussion explores how to map different measures onto segments, questions whether Rainbow is the right tool, and proposes using an entropy-based word score as a preliminary measure while building a prototype that can visualize multiple values.
action_items: Pool the group's understanding of the XML format and its structure
Review sample XML files together to understand the general structure and meanings
Try to map each measure to the existing segment structure
Provide one value per segment for each measure so both can be loaded into a prototype
Work together on an entropy-based score as a preliminary measure
Try to understand the NITE data system better before continuing manual parsing
risks: The group does not yet understand the XML and internal data structure well enough
Rainbow may not be the right tool for the measure they are trying to compute
Manually parsing and combining times and segments may be inefficient and error-prone
open_questions: What is the best way to structure the information density annotation?
Is Rainbow really returning the kind of measure they want?
Can both sets of values be mapped directly onto the existing segments?
Would it be better to combine the two scores in the software instead of the XML file?
